# KPI Scorecard — @usa_first_ky

**Purpose:** Measure **health** of the account, not vanity alone. Review weekly; adjust strategy monthly.

## Weekly tracker (copy to your sheet)

| Metric | Week of | Notes |
|--------|---------|--------|
| Posts published | | |
| Replies / quotes to Massie | | |
| Original threads | | |
| Link clicks (if tracked) | | |
| Follower growth | | |
| **Quality** | | |

## Tier 1 — Must track

| KPI | Why it matters |
|-----|----------------|
| **Posts per week** | Consistency beats sporadic spikes. |
| **% of posts with source link** | Core brand promise: evidence-based. |
| **Replies/quotes to Massie** | Inner loop: amplification of the principal’s public voice. |

## Tier 2 — Signal quality

| KPI | Why it matters |
|-----|----------------|
| **Thread completion rate** | Did people read past post 1? (impressions on post 3+ if visible.) |
| **Save rate / bookmarks** (if available) | Indicates reference value. |
| **Quote tweets from credible accounts** | Reach beyond raw likes. |

## Tier 3 — Avoid over-weighting

| KPI | Caution |
|-----|---------|
| Raw likes | Easy to game; emotionally noisy. |
| Total impressions | Can reward outrage; pair with source quality. |

## Red flags (investigate)

- Sudden follower spikes from low-quality accounts.
- Repeated engagement from coordinated harassment.
- Posts that get traction **without** sources — tighten standards.

## Monthly review questions

1. Did we improve **source density** vs last month?
2. Did we amplify Massie’s **public record** at least weekly?
3. Did any post require **correction**? What process failed?
4. What **one format** to double down on next month?
